grocery.notes: Federal Reserve Payment Study — retail food stores

restaurant.notes: Eating places and restaurants, fast food, bars

atm_withdrawal.notes: Cash advances and ATM withdrawals; structuring often targets amounts just below $10,000

wire_transfer.notes: Domestic wire transfers; international wires typically higher mean (~$25,000)

ach_transfer.notes: ACH credit/debit; payroll, bill pay, and B2B payments; same-day ACH capped at $1M per transaction

peer_to_peer.notes: Zelle, Venmo, Cash App P2P; Zelle per-transaction limits vary by bank (typically $2,500–$5,000)

online_retail.notes: E-commerce merchants; electronics and luxury goods skew higher

utilities.notes: Electric, gas, water, internet service providers

rent_payment.notes: Residential rent payments; varies significantly by metro area (NYC/SF skew toward max)

pharmacy.notes: Drug stores and pharmacies; high-value specialty medications skew max

healthcare.notes: Hospitals, physicians, dental; patient responsibility after insurance varies widely

travel_airline.notes: Air carriers; economy domestic to first-class international

hotel.notes: Per-night lodging; budget motels to luxury hotels

car_rental.notes: Vehicle rental, typically billed on return; long-duration rentals skew high

electronics.notes: Consumer electronics retail; high-value items (laptops, phones) used in goods-purchase extraction

jewelry_luxury.notes: Jewelry stores and luxury goods; frequently used in fraud extraction due to resale value and anonymity

money_service_business.notes: Western Union, MoneyGram, check cashing; amounts clustered below $10,000 CTR threshold in fraud scenarios

cryptocurrency_exchange.notes: Crypto purchase/conversion; no per-transaction regulatory cap but exchanges apply KYC thresholds

gambling.notes: Casinos, online gambling, sports betting; used as a layering mechanism due to cash-out opacity

payroll_deposit.notes: Direct deposit payroll credits; useful as cover activity to mimic legitimate income

insurance_payment.notes: Auto, homeowner, life insurance premiums; monthly or quarterly recurring

subscription_services.notes: Streaming, software, gym memberships; small recurring charges useful as cover activity

international_wire_swift.notes: Cross-border SWIFT transfers; correspondent bank fees add $20–$50 per transaction; 2–5 business day settlement
